page.content: In the study by Shah et al. [106] conducted in 1991 involving 7 PD patients, the antioxidant used was 100 mg ascorbic acid/day over a study period of 4 weeks, resulting in ↑ 45% in serum ascorbic acid levels. Ando et al. [100] in 1999 studied 16 CAPD patients with 1.8 gr EPA/day for 12 weeks, which led to ↓ serum ox-LDL levels. Islam et al. [101] in 2000 examined 17 PD patients, administering 800 IU AT/day for 12 weeks, resulting in ↓ susceptibility of LDL oxidation, ↑ 217% serum lipid-standardized AT, and ↑ 135% serum LDL AT levels. O' Byrne et al. [104] in 2001 also studied 17 PD patients with 800 IU AT/day for 12 weeks, achieving ↑ serum LDL AT levels with no effect on autoantibodies to ox-LDL. Diepeveen et al. [102] in 2005 researched 21 PD patients receiving 40 mg atorvastatin/day over 12 weeks, resulting in ↓ serum ox-LDL levels, improved lipid profile, and no effect on in vitro LDL oxidizability. Another study in 2005 with 800 IU AT/day over 12 weeks noted no effect on serum ox-LDL levels, no effect on lipid profile, and ↓ in vitro LDL oxidizability. Domenici et al.

lines.text: In the study by Shah et al. [106] conducted in 1991 involving 7 PD patients, the antioxidant used was 100 mg ascorbic acid/day
over a study period of 4 weeks, resulting in ↑ 45% in serum ascorbic acid levels. Ando et al. [100] in 1999 studied 16 CAPD
patients with 1.8 gr EPA/day for 12 weeks, which led to ↓ serum ox-LDL levels. Islam et al. [101] in 2000 examined 17 PD
patients, administering 800 IU AT/day for 12 weeks, resulting in ↓ susceptibility of LDL oxidation, ↑ 217% serum lipid-
standardized AT, and ↑ 135% serum LDL AT levels. O' Byrne et al. [104] in 2001 also studied 17 PD patients with 800 IU
AT/day for 12 weeks, achieving ↑ serum LDL AT levels with no effect on autoantibodies to ox-LDL. Diepeveen et al. [102] in
2005 researched 21 PD patients receiving 40 mg atorvastatin/day over 12 weeks, resulting in ↓ serum ox-LDL levels, improved
lipid profile, and no effect on in vitro LDL oxidizability. Another study in 2005 with 800 IU AT/day over 12 weeks noted no
effect on serum ox-LDL levels, no effect on lipid profile, and ↓ in vitro LDL oxidizability. Domenici et al.

page.content: [103] in 2005 with 22 PD patients taking 300 mg AT x3/week for 4 weeks saw ↑ 50% serum vitamin E levels, ↓ protein oxidation, and ↓ OS-induced DNA damage (serum levels 8-OHdG). Uzum et al. [23] in 2006 studied 13 PD patients with 300 mg AT/day for 20 weeks, resulting in ↑ serum vitamin E levels and ↓ erythrocyte osmotic fragility. Nascimento et al. [117] in 2010 involved 30 PD patients with 1200 mg NAC/day for 8 weeks, leading to ↑ serum NAC levels (2.6 to 24.8 mmol/l) and ↓ serum IL-6 levels (9.4 to 7.6 pg/ml). Lastly, Boudouris et al. [15] in 2013 examined 20 CAPD patients with 250 mg vitamin C/day and 400 IU AT/day for 8 weeks, resulting in ↑ serum, urine, and peritoneal fluid TAC levels and ↓ serum, urine, and peritoneal fluid MDA levels.
